channel.title: Data Breach & Ransomware Aggregator
channel.description: Real-time data breach notifications from state registries, federal sources, and ransomware trackers

item.title: Anywhere Real Estate Inc.
item.description:
Anywhere Real Estate Inc. reported a state registry affecting 17,429 records in Madison, NJ.
📊 Records Affected: 17,429 | 📍 Location: Madison, NJ | 📰 Source: Maine AG | 🏷️ Type: State Registry

item.title: Volvo Group North America, LLC
item.description:
Volvo Group North America, LLC reported a state registry affecting 16991 records in Greensboro, NC.
📊 Records Affected: 16991 | 📍 Location: Greensboro, NC | 📰 Source: Maine AG | 🏷️ Type: State Registry

item.title: After major Poland energy grid cyberattack, CISA issues warning to U.S. audience
item.description:
The Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency said the attack highlighted threats from vulnerable edge devices to operational technology and industrial control systems. The post After major Poland energy grid cyberattack, CISA issues warning to U.S. audience appeared first on CyberScoop.
📰 Source: CyberScoop | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Microsoft Patch Tuesday matches last year’s zero-day high with six actively exploited vulnerabilities
item.description:
Microsoft said three of the exploited vulnerabilities were publicly known, suggesting attackers already had details about the defects prior to Tuesday’s release. The post Microsoft Patch Tuesday matches last year’s zero-day high with six actively exploited vulnerabilities appeared first on CyberScoop.
📰 Source: CyberScoop | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: 0APT ransomware group rises swiftly with bluster, along with genuine threat of attack
item.description:
Most signs suggest the group is running a massive hoax by claiming hundreds of initial victims, but at least some of the threat 0APT poses is grounded in truth backed by proven capabilities. The post 0APT ransomware group rises swiftly with bluster, along with genuine threat of attack appeared first on CyberScoop.
📰 Source: CyberScoop | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Shai-hulud: The Hidden Costs of Supply Chain Attacks
item.description:
Recent supply chain attacks involving self-propagating worms have spread far, but the damage and long-term impact is hard to quantify.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: 'Reynolds' Bundles BYOVD With Ransomware Payload
item.description:
Researchers discovered a newly disclosed vulnerable driver embedded in Reynolds' ransomware, illustrating the increasing popularity of the defense-evasion technique.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Warlock Gang Breaches SmarterTools Via SmarterMail Bugs
item.description:
The ransomware group breached SmarterTools through a vulnerability in the company's own SmarterMail product.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: OT Attacks Get Scary With 'Living-off-the-Plant' Techniques
item.description:
Ironically, security by obscurity has helped prevent dangerous OT attacks in recent years. It won't be that way forever.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: How to Stay on Top of Future Threats With a Cutting-Edge SOC
item.description:
CISOs should focus on harnessing and securing AI and building new skills among their people. Vision and change management can transform security.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Microsoft Patches 6 Actively Exploited Zero-Days
item.description:
Three of those zero-days are security feature bypass flaws, which give attackers a way to slip past built-in protections in multiple Microsoft products.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: SolarWinds WHD Attacks Highlight Risks of Exposed Apps
item.description:
Organizations that have exposed their instances of Web Help Desk to the public Internet have inadvertently made them prime targets for attackers.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Top Cyber Industry Defenses Spike CO2 Emissions
item.description:
Organizations can improve their climate footprints by optimizing two specific cybersecurity protections, without incurring added risks.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: AI Rising: Do We Know Enough About the Data Populating It?
item.description:
Organizations remain reluctant to address the fact that AI can dangerously expose business operations as well as personal data.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Hacker Conversations: Professional Hacker Douglas Day
item.description:
Day became a professional hacker by choice. But that doesn’t mean he isn’t a natural hacker. The post Hacker Conversations: Professional Hacker Douglas Day appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Security in the Dark: Recognizing the Signs of Hidden Information
item.description:
Security failures don’t always start with attackers, sometimes they start with missing truth. The post Security in the Dark: Recognizing the Signs of Hidden Information appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Zast.AI Raises $6 Million for AI-Powered Code Security
item.description:
The startup relies on AI agents to identify software vulnerabilities and validate them before reporting. The post Zast.AI Raises $6 Million for AI-Powered Code Security appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Conduent Breach Hits Volvo Group: Nearly 17,000 Employees’ Data Exposed
item.description:
The Conduent data breach affects at least 25 million individuals, up from 10 million estimated a few months ago. The post Conduent Breach Hits Volvo Group: Nearly 17,000 Employees’ Data Exposed appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: GitGuardian Raises $50 Million for Secrets and Non-Human Identity Security
item.description:
The secrets security company has raised more than $100 million since its creation in 2017. The post GitGuardian Raises $50 Million for Secrets and Non-Human Identity Security appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Webinar Today: Identity Under Attack – Strengthen Your Identity Defenses
item.description:
Gain practical insights on balancing security, user experience, and operational efficiency while staying ahead of increasingly sophisticated threats. The post Webinar Today: Identity Under Attack – Strengthen Your Identity Defenses appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Nevada Unveils New Statewide Data Classification Policy Months After Cyberattack
item.description:
Officials said data will now be classified as one of four categories: “public,” “sensitive,” “confidential” or “restricted.” The post Nevada Unveils New Statewide Data Classification Policy Months After Cyberattack appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Cyber Command, NSA nominee Rudd advances to Senate floor
item.description:
The Senate Armed Services Committee, which shares jurisdiction over the nomination due to the “dual-hat” leadership structure that governs both entities, approved Rudd by voice vote last month.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Georgia healthcare company data breach impacts more than 620,000
item.description:
The company told victims in September about the breach, and said an investigation revealed hackers were in ApolloMD’s IT environment between May 22 and May 23.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Interim CISA chief: ‘When the government shuts down, cyber threats do not’
item.description:
A shutdown would “degrade our capacity to provide timely and actionable guidance to help partners defend their networks,” acting CISA Director Madhu Gottumukkala told the House Appropriations Homeland Security subcommittee.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Dismantling Defenses: Trump 2.0 Cyber Year in Review
item.description:
The Trump administration has pursued a staggering range of policy pivots this past year that threaten to weaken the nation’s ability and willingness to address a broad spectrum of technology challenges, from cybersecurity and privacy to countering disinformation, fraud and corruption. These shifts, along with the president’s efforts to restrict free speech and freedom of the press, have come at such a rapid clip that many readers probably aren’t even aware of them all.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Happy 16th Birthday, KrebsOnSecurity.com!
item.description:
KrebsOnSecurity.com celebrates its 16th anniversary today! A huge "thank you" to all of our readers -- newcomers, long-timers and drive-by critics alike. Your engagement this past year here has been tremendous and truly a salve on a handful of dark days. Happily, comeuppance was a strong theme running through our coverage in 2025, with a primary focus on entities that enabled complex and globally-dispersed cybercrime services.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: PrintForm
item.description:
NOTE: No files or stolen information are exfiltrated, downloaded, taken, hosted, seen, reposted, or disclosed...
🎭 Threat Actor: SPACEBEARS | 📰 Source: Red Packet Security | 🏷️ Type: Ransomware

item.title: Volvo Group North America customer data exposed in Conduent hack
item.description:
Volvo Group North America disclosed that it suffered an indirect data breach stemming from the compromise of IT systems at American business services giant Conduent, of which Volvo is a customer. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: North Korean hackers use new macOS malware in crypto-theft attacks
item.description:
North Korean hackers are running tailored campaigns using AI-generated video and the ClickFix technique to deliver malware for macOS and Windows to targets in the cryptocurrency sector. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Crazy ransomware gang abuses employee monitoring tool in attacks
item.description:
A member of the Crazy ransomware gang is abusing legitimate employee monitoring software and the SimpleHelp remote support tool to maintain persistence in corporate networks, evade detection, and prepare for ransomware deployment. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Apple fixes zero-day flaw used in 'extremely sophisticated' attacks
item.description:
Apple has released security updates to fix a zero-day vulnerability that was exploited in an "extremely sophisticated attack" targeting specific individuals. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: FCC Cybersecurity Alert and Recommendations to Communications Providers
item.description:
On January 29, 2026, the FCC issued public notice DA 26-96: PUBLIC SAFETY AND HOMELAND SECURITY BUREAU HIGHLIGHTS BEST PRACTICES FOR DEFENDING AGAINST RANSOMWARE ATTACKS By this Public Notice, the Public Safety and Homeland Security Bureau (Bureau) of the Federal Communications Commission (Commission) urges communications providers to implement cybersecurity best practices to protect their networks... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Romania’s oil pipeline operator confirms cyberattack as hackers claim data theft
item.description:
Daryna Antoniuk reports: Romania’s national oil pipeline operator Conpet said a cyberattack disrupted parts of its technology infrastructure and knocked its website offline earlier this week, adding that oil transport operations were not affected. Conpet, which operates about 3,800 kilometers (2,360 miles) of pipelines supplying domestic and imported crude oil and petroleum products to refineries... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Hackers Use Signal QR Codes to Spy on Military and Political Leaders
item.description:
Deeba Ahmed reports: Security experts in Germany are on high alert following a wave of digital attacks aimed at high-ranking officials and public figures. The warning comes from the country’s top security bodies- the Federal Office for Information Security (BSI) and the Federal Office for the Protection of the Constitution (BfV). They have discovered that... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Discord to start requiring face scan or ID to access adult content
item.description:
Liv McMahon reports: Discord will soon require users globally to verify their age with a face scan or by uploading a form of ID if they want to access adult content. […] Users can either upload a photo of their ID to confirm their age or take a video selfie, where AI will be used... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: CodeRED Emergency Notification System Incident Update
item.description:
It’s easy to forget that just because a news cycle may be about 15 minutes, incident response is much longer. The following is an update from St. Mary’s County in Maryland about the CodeRED emergency systems ransomware attack that affected numerous EMS systems throughout the country. The attack had occurred in CodeRED’s legacy OnSolve environment... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Hacktivist scrapes over 500,000 stalkerware customers’ payment records
item.description:
Some may experience some schadenfraude over this one. Zack Whittaker reports: A hacktivist has scraped more than half-a-million payment records from a provider of consumer-grade “stalkerware” phone surveillance apps, exposing the email addresses and partial payment information of customers who paid to spy on others. The transactions contain records of payments for phone-tracking services like... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Singapore says China-backed hackers targeted its four largest phone companies
item.description:
Zack Whittaker reports: Singapore’s government has blamed a known Chinese cyber-espionage group for targeting four of its top telecommunication companies as part of a months-long attack. In a statement Monday, Singapore confirmed for the first time that the hackers, known as UNC3886, targeted the country’s telecoms infrastructure, including its largest companies: Singtel, StarHub, M1, and Simba... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: South Korea government is promoting bill for statutory damages for personal information leaks.
item.description:
Kwon Soon-wan reports: The Democratic Party of Korea and the government are pushing for a bill that would hold companies liable for damages in personal information leaks even in the absence of intent or negligence. It was reported on the 10th that the ruling and government parties intend to apply this law retroactively to past... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Maine House Passes Strong Privacy Bill
item.description:
EPIC writes: A strong comprehensive privacy bill passed the Maine House of Representatives today. The bill, LD 1822, closely mirrors the privacy law Maryland passed in 2024 and would extend essential privacy protections to Mainers. The bill includes strong data minimization requirements, enhanced protections for sensitive data, and civil rights protections prohibiting data-driven discrimination. EPIC has testified in support of... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Cybersecurity Information Sharing Act of 2015 Reauthorized Through September 2026
item.description:
Ashden Fein, Jess Gonzalez Valenzuela, Analese Bridges, John Webster Leslie, and Claire O’Rourke of Covington and Burling write: The Cybersecurity Information Sharing Act of 2015 (“CISA 2015”), which provides liability protections and other safeguards for sharing certain cybersecurity information with the U.S. federal government and private entities, was reauthorized as part of the funding bill... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: DOCS Dermatology Group; Center for Neuropsychology and Learning Disclose Data Breaches
item.description:
Central States Dermatology Services (DOCS Dermatology Group) in Ohio and The Center for Neuropsychology and Learning in Michigan have identified […] The post DOCS Dermatology Group; Center for Neuropsychology and Learning Disclose Data Breaches appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: What Is Healthcare-Adjacent Data?
item.description:
Healthcare-adjacent data is any health‑related or health‑influenced information that falls outside HIPAA’s definition of Protected Health Information because it is […] The post What Is Healthcare-Adjacent Data? appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Jefferson-Blount-St. Clair Mental Health Authority Data Breach Affects 30,000 Patients
item.description:
Jefferson-Blount-St. Clair Mental Health Authority in Alabama, Cottage Hospital in New Hampshire, WindRose Health Network in Indiana, and Iroquois Memorial […] The post Jefferson-Blount-St. Clair Mental Health Authority Data Breach Affects 30,000 Patients appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: McLaren Health Care Pays $14 Million to Settle Litigation Over Ransomware Attacks
item.description:
McLaren Health Care has agreed to pay $14 million to settle class action litigation stemming from two ransomware attacks in […] The post McLaren Health Care Pays $14 Million to Settle Litigation Over Ransomware Attacks appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Precipio; Pit River Health Service; Tulane University Medical Group Confirm Data Breaches
item.description:
Data breaches have been announced by the Connecticut diagnostic laboratory Precipio, Pit River Health Service in California, and Tulane University […] The post Precipio; Pit River Health Service; Tulane University Medical Group Confirm Data Breaches appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Staten Island University Hospital Settles Lawsuit Over Business Associate Data Breach
item.description:
Staten Island University Hospital (SIUH) in New York has agreed to settle a class action lawsuit over a 2024 data […] The post Staten Island University Hospital Settles Lawsuit Over Business Associate Data Breach appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: 83,000 Clients Affected by Cyberattack on Ohio Counseling Center
item.description:
The Counseling Center of Wayne and Holmes Counties has experienced a cyberattack affecting 83,350 individuals. Data breaches have also been […] The post 83,000 Clients Affected by Cyberattack on Ohio Counseling Center appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Three Healthcare Providers Settle Class Action Data Breach Lawsuits
item.description:
Settlements have received preliminary approval from the courts to resolve class action lawsuits against Northeast Rehabilitation Hospital Network, American Addiction […] The post Three Healthcare Providers Settle Class Action Data Breach Lawsuits appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Nuna Baby Essentials, Inc.
